Store a version number with each record. Increment it on every successful write. At commit time, check if the version you read matches the current version - if not, another transaction modified the data.
Buffer all writes in a transaction-local writeSet Map. Only apply them to the actual data store during commit, after validating that no conflicts exist. This ensures atomicity.

For conflict detection, maintain a readSet that tracks which keys were read and at what version. During commit validation, iterate through the readSet and compare each version against the current version in the store.

Implement withTransaction() as a retry loop. If commit fails due to conflict, create a new transaction and re-execute the function with fresh data. Limit retries (e.g., max 3) to prevent infinite loops on hot keys.
